input_prompt: Review this month for duplicate invoice risk.

top_drivers.description: Two invoices share the same vendor, amount, and invoice reference.

title: Duplicate Invoice

input_prompt: Look for split payments under approval thresholds.

top_drivers.description: Multiple payments to the same vendor were posted just below the approval threshold.

title: Split Payments Under Threshold

input_prompt: Check whether one vendor is concentrating too much spend.

top_drivers.description: One vendor represents an unusually high share of monthly spend.

title: Vendor Concentration

input_prompt: Find round-dollar payment patterns that may need review.

top_drivers.description: Several large payments were posted as exact round-dollar amounts.

title: Round Dollar Payments

input_prompt: Review reconciliation mismatches for possible risk.

top_drivers.description: Bank and ledger records do not match for a reviewed payment.

title: Reconciliation Mismatch

input_prompt: Check for ghost vendor fraud risk this month.

top_drivers.description: A vendor with no verifiable business registration or physical address received two payments totalling $25,000. The vendor has no tax ID on file and onboarding was never completed.

title: Ghost Vendor

input_prompt: Are any employees also registered as vendors? Check for fraud risk.

top_drivers.description: An Accounts Payable employee shares a tax ID with an active vendor and has self-approved both vendor payments this period totalling $16,800.

title: Employee Vendor Overlap

input_prompt: Find vendors sharing the same bank account - possible fraud risk.

top_drivers.driver: Shared bank account used by multiple vendors
top_drivers.description: Two vendors with different legal names and registrations route payments to the same bank account, totalling $85,500 this period. This pattern is consistent with shell company or controlled-entity fraud.

title: Shared Bank Account Multiple Vendors

input_prompt: Were any vendors paid before their onboarding was completed? Fraud risk review.

top_drivers.description: A payment of $22,000 was issued to a vendor whose onboarding process had not been started at the time of payment, bypassing vendor validation controls.

title: Vendor Paid Before Onboarding

input_prompt: Look for duplicate vendor name variations that may indicate fraud risk.

top_drivers.description: Two vendor records with nearly identical names, different tax IDs, and different bank accounts received identical payment amounts on the same date. This pattern is consistent with duplicate-vendor fraud.

title: Duplicate Vendor Name Variation

input_prompt: Are there unusual refund patterns that may indicate fraud risk?

top_drivers.description: Two refund credits exceed the original invoice amount and were routed to a bank account not on file for this vendor. Total refund exposure is $25,200.

title: Unusual Refund Activity

input_prompt: Review payroll for anomalies and signs of fraud risk.

top_drivers.description: A terminated employee received two payroll disbursements totalling $11,600 after their termination date of 2026-03-31. Payments were routed to an external account added after termination.

title: Payroll Anomaly

input_prompt: Are payments processed on weekends or after hours? Check for fraud risk.

top_drivers.description: Two large payments totalling $59,500 were processed on Sundays between 1-3 AM, outside of normal business hours and approval windows.

title: Weekend After Hours Payment

input_prompt: Are any invoices being split to stay under approval thresholds? Fraud risk check.

top_drivers.description: Five invoices of $19,800 each were posted to the same vendor on the same date across separate purchase orders. The combined total of $99,000 requires CFO approval, but each individual invoice falls just below the $20,000 manager approval threshold.

title: Approval Threshold Splitting

input_prompt: Has vendor concentration risk been increasing over time? Review for fraud.

top_drivers.description: A single vendor's share of total spend increased from 9% in March to 27% in April to 65% in May. This rapid concentration growth is an indicator of potential fraudulent diversion or kickback arrangements.

title: High Risk Vendor Concentration Over Time

input_prompt: Synthesize vendor risk and entity relationships for possible conflict risk.

top_drivers.description: Vendor-risk and entity-relationship services returned linked employee-vendor overlap evidence for the same vendor.

title: Phase 4 Vendor Entity Overlap Synthesis

input_prompt: Synthesize reconciliation mismatches and threshold-splitting signals.

top_drivers.driver: Approval threshold splitting with reconciliation mismatch
top_drivers.description: Threshold splitting and reconciliation mismatch evidence share the same vendor and payment batch.

reconciliation_risk.recommended_next_action: Review unmatched bank and ledger records.

title: Phase 4 Reconciliation Threshold Synthesis

input_prompt: Synthesize rapid vendor onboarding and round-dollar payment risk.

top_drivers.description: A recently onboarded vendor received several exact round-dollar payments.

triggered_rules: new vendor paid before full validation
vendor_risk.recommended_next_action: Validate onboarding records before payment review.

title: Phase 4 Rapid Onboarding Round Dollar Synthesis

input_prompt: Synthesize duplicate vendor and shared account indicators.

top_drivers.driver: Duplicate vendor with shared bank account
top_drivers.description: Duplicate vendor records route payments to the same account indicator.

entity_relationship_risk.recommended_next_action: Validate account ownership and vendor master changes.

title: Phase 4 Duplicate Vendor Shared Account Synthesis

input_prompt: Synthesize vendor and entity signals, but suppress unsupported correlations.

top_drivers.driver: High vendor risk and separate employee-vendor overlap signals
top_drivers.description: The deterministic services returned elevated vendor risk and employee-vendor overlap signals, but the evidence anchors are unrelated.

title: Phase 4 Unsupported Correlation Suppression

input_prompt: Synthesize multiple medium deterministic risk signals into an investigation priority.

top_drivers.description: Vendor review, reconciliation review, and payment review each returned medium deterministic concern without a stronger direct correlation.

vendor_risk.recommended_next_action: Review vendor risk if other domains also remain elevated.

title: Phase 4 Multiple Medium Risk Elevation
